attacking_style: Opportunistic late-race attacks in sprint stages, going solo inside final 4km to disrupt organized leadouts and force sprinter teams to chase.
team_tactics: Operates independently from traditional leadout trains, exploiting moments when sprinter teams are positioning to launch surprise moves.
terrain_preference: Sprint-friendly stages where organized leadouts are expected, allowing tactical disruption rather than pure power advantages.
vulnerability: Single observation limits vulnerability assessment; likely exposed when teams maintain organized chase with fresh domestiques.
key_patterns: Attacks inside final 4km on sprint stages to disrupt leadout organization
Shows opportunistic racing instincts rather than committing to traditional sprinter support roles
Forces sprinter teams into reactive mode by attacking before leadout positioning complete
form_trajectory: unknown: single observation from 2017 insufficient to assess trajectory, though showed positive attacking intent
